title: Constraint-Based Manuscript
abstract: Primary anchor manuscript detailing the constraint model.

title: Framework Primer
abstract: Working framework notes that map the core alignment concepts.

title: Quiet Systems Essay
abstract: Short essay on quiet system design and constraint-aware research.

title: When Inner Life Becomes Performance
abstract: How inward realities become external targets, and why measuring internal alignment can distort the very thing it claims to reveal.

title: Gatekept Clarity
abstract: A structural distinction between guidance that increases discernment and systems that preserve dependence by controlling access to understanding.

title: The Bible Keeps Pointing to the Inside
abstract: A reading of scripture's repeated movement from outward legibility to inward reality, with special attention to heart, fruit, hypocrisy, and truth.

title: Low Agency Increases Steerability
abstract: Why reduced capacity makes persons easier to govern through fear, identity, pressure, and externally supplied certainty.

title: False Religion Beyond Church
abstract: A broader account of false religion as any sacred external order that binds people to constructed reality instead of deeper truth.

title: When Systems Replace Truth
abstract: How representations, metrics, roles, and official forms gradually become more important than the realities they were meant to serve.

title: Why Self-Help Feels Hollow
abstract: An examination of partial clarity, monetized suffering, and why so much self-help feels helpful and false at the same time.

title: Why Performance Is Easier Than Transformation
abstract: Why visible compliance scales faster than inward reordering, and why systems so often settle for performance over real change.

title: The Difference Between a Guide and a Gatekeeper
abstract: A distinction between relationships that restore agency and structures that keep themselves necessary as mediators of reality.

title: Reality Fragmented and Sold Back
abstract: How modern life breaks truth into branded fragments, distributes them as insight, and leaves people informed but under-integrated.

title: Core Axioms
abstract: Compressed starting assumptions underlying the Alignment Theory framework.

title: Core Laws
abstract: Law-like structural statements describing recurring dynamics across persons, systems, and culture.

title: Biblical Grammar of Alignment Theory
abstract: Scriptural anchor translations linking biblical language to the framework's structural distinctions.

title: Metaphysical Claims
abstract: Largest claims of the framework concerning reality, truth, evil, meaning, salvation, and Christ.

title: Capacity Check-In
abstract: Instrument concept for assessing capacity and constraint readiness.

title: Case Notes: Alignment Drift
abstract: Observational notes documenting drift patterns and recovery signals.
